title: Level 828
wikitext: {{CSS|content= { transform: scale(1.05); } .fandom-community-header__image img { opacity: 0; } .fandom-community-header__image:before { content: ''; background: url(https://static.wikia.nocookie.net/backrooms/images/e/ea/Diamond.png/revision/latest?cb=20260315175727) no-repeat; margin: 0.9rem 0 0rem 0rem; width: 100px; height: 112px; background-size: 97px; position: absolute; transition: 0s; } }} {{DISPLAYTITLE: Level 828: "Fortune of Misfortune"}}__NOTOC__{{Modern Theme}}{{Blurbackground|file=crystal 831.png|opacity=56}}{{Audio|align=center|file=Level 831 music.mp3|secrettext=FORTUNE OF MISFORTUNE|loop=yes}}{{Image Banner|image=Crystal 831 2.png|caption=My precious...}}{{Alt Class 2|no=828|entity=2|environment=4|exit=4}} '''Level 828''' is the 829th ore of the [[Backrooms Wiki|Anomaly Mine]]. == Overview == '''Level 828''', colloquially referred to as "'''The Fortune of Misfortune'''", resembles a series of infinitely expanding, interconnected chambers made of steel, which are roughly 30 m² each and have a hexagonal shape. These chambers have lights hanging from the ceiling on chains, and said lights grow dimmer the further one strays from the center. Each chamber is connected by short metallic tunnels that have no ores in them. All chambers within '''Level 828''' are filled with various precious minerals, primarily '''Amethyst''', '''Diamond''', '''Lapis Lazuli,''' and '''Gold'''. Each of these minerals can be found under diverse circumstances and conditions.{{Blurimage|file=Ore close-up.png|caption=An image of various ores recovered from Level 828.}}Other, lesser-known minerals have been reported to be present in the far reaches of '''Level 828''', such as '''Ruby''', '''Sapphire''', and '''Bronze'''. While the minerals and ores of '''Level 828''' can be found in diverse locations and under various conditions, pattern analysis has identified specific areas where they are most commonly found. The analysis is listed below: *'''Amethyst''' deposits are often found in isolated chambers where little to no other ores are present. This mineral is primarily present in the form of geodes and raw ores and can easily injure human limbs due to the sharpness of its form and materials. *'''Diamonds''' appear scattered throughout all chambers in most cases. This noteworthy and valuable ore is found in the shape of a polished gem. *'''Lapis Lazuli''' is an extremely rare mineral within '''Level 828''', being difficult to find and appearing in minimal quantities. This mineral is found within the level's expanse either in the form of powder or jewelry, and has a high trading value due to its rarity not solely in '''Level 828''' but also throughout [[Level List|the Wake]]. *'''Gold''' is the most commonly found valuable ore in '''Level 828'''; it has a chance of appearing in every chamber and is typically found in high quantities. This ore is found in a multitude of forms, including but not limited to coins, rings, and ingots. It is said that instances of [[Object 1]] in the form of bottles can occasionally be found in the chambers of '''Level 828''', though an official publication confirming these rumors is yet to be released. The overall temperature of '''Level 828''''s chambers fluctuates between 30 and 50 degrees Celsius (86 and 122 degrees Fahrenheit); however, some chambers have natural heating systems that can increase the temperature to 162ºC, an exceptionally high temperature for the human body to handle. Lastly, '''Level 828''' has been deemed stable, with little to no environmentally altering phenomena such as [[Phenomenon 14]]. However, it is presumed that this renders exits few in number and difficult to locate. == Chamber Types == '''Level 828''''s chambers come in various types. All documented and researched chamber types are listed below. === Standard Chambers === As the name suggests, standard chambers are the most common and first discovered type of chamber within '''Level 828'''. Each instance of a standard chamber contains little to no difference, excluding the shape of the chamber and the positions of its components. Furthermore, all non-exclusive ores and minerals within '''Level 828''' can only be discovered in standard chambers. And lastly, this chamber type does not present any unique phenomena or anomalies, other than those that the level itself contains throughout its expanse. === Memory Chambers === ''Memory Chambers'' are a rare type of chamber in '''Level 828''' composed entirely of a unique mineral, called ''Memory Crystal''. These strange ores are unique solely to '''Level 828''', though they have been found to contain numerous structural similarities to quartz from [[The Frontrooms]]. ==== Memory Crystals ==== {{Object Class|risk=4|properties=Solid / Anomalous}} The appearance of an average Memory Crystal comes in the form of a purple crystal with a faint, lavender-hued glow. Separating a crystal into fractions does not diminish the glow, but rather spreads it to all of said pieces. Strangely enough, when two separate Memory Crystals are brought closely together, their glows seem to steadily connect with each other, as if the pieces have formed an invisible bond of some sort. Analysts presume that if kept together long enough, the pieces may collide into one on their own; however, an official publication confirming or denying this is yet to be released. The central power of Memory Crystals is their ability to steadily absorb the memories of anyone within a one-meter radius. This is indeed to say that the crystals are "stealing" from the victim, not merely duplicating the victim's memories. Once a crystal has absorbed most of a victim's memories, it will lose its absorption power and instead begin depicting the affected's memories in its form. As a result of its strange abilities, the Memory Crystal is quite a popular and valuable item according to the trade market of [[The Backrooms]]. Attempts to melt a memory crystal will result in a liquid that has a purple hue; this liquid, called ''Memory Fluid'', is considered a variant of memory crystals. Memory Fluid is additionally deemed somewhat more dangerous than its parent object, as the liquid presents the same risks but with a much more potent ability to affect individuals. Memory Fluid immediately attacks anyone who interacts with it in any shape or form, resulting in rapid absorption even when the individual withdraws from the interaction. Due to these dangers, it has been officially named a ''biological weapon'' and condemned by the vast majority of [[Groups|organizations]]. Upon the vaporization of memory fluid, one will be presented with ''Memory Gas''. This is considered a somewhat more dangerous variant, as the other two previously listed variants are easier to avoid. Memory Gas itself manifests as a purple-tinted vapor; the increased risks come from the fact that its effects are activated when one inhales the gas in any way, effectively making it an airborne virus. As of currently, no research or testing has been able to be successful in creating an antidote or cure for Memory Crystals; furthermore, in no cases of a Memory Crystal's effects taking place have a victim's memories ever been recovered. All victims either continued living with severe brain damage affecting their cognitive ability or later lost their lives to Alzheimer's disease. === Empty Chambers === The third and final type of chamber that can be found within '''Level 828''' is the ''Empty Chamber''. Empty chambers are currently the most difficult type of chamber to come across, and are the only documented way of finding egress from '''Level 828'''. As the name suggests, empty chambers do not contain any of the aforementioned ores, nor can they contain instances of [[Object 1]]. Furthermore, empty chambers bear a somewhat more aged appearance, and various areas and parts of empty chambers often contain cracks in their structure. Other than the previously stated properties, empty chambers possess no additional noteworthy features. == Addendum == {{Phenomenon Class|frequency=3|effectiveness=3|danger=4|logo=[[File:Diamond.png|150 px|center]]}} '''Level 828''' possesses a strange and unique phenomenon. Named ''Greed'', this is an anomaly that manifests in psychological form and is present throughout '''Level 828'''. As the name suggests, ''Greed'' results in increased obsession over '''Level 828's''' ores and minerals for the affected individual(s), which intensifies with time. {{Blurimage|file=Symbolic greed.png|caption=A symbolic and stylized image of a wanderer affected by ''Greed''.}} While the cause for ''Greed'' has not yet been fully determined, it is established that the phenomenon can get very dangerous the longer one remains within '''Level 828'''; this is because every case in which the phenomenon was reported (which consist of about 70% of all recorded individual experiences in '''Level 828'''), the phenomenon hijacked the victim's mind and inputted several unnatural thoughts which convinced the mind of certain false features that the level's minerals present. Common thoughts reported by survivors of the phenomenon are listed below: * Thoughts claiming that memory crystals are the key to exiting '''Level 828'''. * Ideas revolving around the ores and minerals having the ability to grant bearers special powers. * Mental resolutions deeming the ores as the victim's property, which all others are apparently attempting to steal from them. * Thinking patterns explaining that '''Level 828''' contains spiritual or symbolic significance/influence towards oneself. * Delusions of grandeur and overt self-importance. The longer one is affected by ''Greed'', the more dangerous it becomes. Over time, if an individual is unable or unwilling to rid themselves of the phenomenon, it typically transitions into [[Phenomenon 35|insanity]]. Currently, the most effective way to avoid capitulation to ''Greed'' is through the use of [[Object 1]], as well as directing focus towards the more negative properties of '''Level 828'''. This is, however, generalized. Avoidance and resistance can vary across different minds and characters. Any individuals who overcome the phenomenon will end its control over them; however, those who succumb are, for the most part, [[Phenomenon 35|doomed]]. == Inhabitants == '''Level 828''' is occupied by two entities. One is [[Entity 3]], an entity species commonly present throughout [[Backrooms Wiki|the Wake]], which lurks in the dark outskirts of the level; however, '''Level 828''' also possesses a single-species entity unique to its expanse. === Enrico === {{Entity Class|Num=UND|Hab=Level 828|Agg=4|Dan=5|Int=4|no=Enrico}} '''Enrico''' is a level-unique entity; simply put, it is a heavily armed instance of [[Entity 9]], except that it shows numerous signs and symptoms of ''Greed'' as well as Stage IV of [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]]. All encounters with the entity should be avoided to the best of one's ability. {{Blurimage|file=Enrico+.webp|caption=A Wanderer's sketch of Enrico}} '''Enrico''' is described as an adult [[Entity 9|Faceling]] resembling a middle-aged Caucasian male. It wears a bulletproof vest, accompanied by a military soldier outfit. When revealed, numerous parts of its skin have tears and rips, assumed to be from [[Phenomenon 35]]. Furthermore, it is typically found in possession of a firearm, which it either has held in its hands or hanging from its back, ready for use. Numerous journal entries from diaries that were previously '''Enrico''''s possession show that it was an actual human being, and a citizen of the [[M.E.G.|Major Explorer Group]]. During this time, it had lived a peaceful life within the ''Outpost Sundial'' of [[Level 242]]. However, '''Enrico''' would later be sent here from [[The Void]] after no-clipping into a steel wall. '''Enrico''' would later fall asleep on the surface of '''Level 828'''; however, it is presumed that '''Enrico''' fell asleep on top of a specific area occupied by underground natural thermal vents. Due to the extreme temperatures in the ground below it and how frequently '''Enrico''' slept in the same spot day after day, its facial features would, over time, begin breaking down and fading away; eventually, it transformed into an instance of [[Entity 9]]. Not much is known about '''Enrico''''s life after its transformation, as almost all subsequent entries are lost, save for Journal Entry 75. What has been determined is that '''Enrico''' most likely became affected by ''Greed'' shortly after losing its face, which would later lead to it succumbing to [[Phenomenon 35]]. As of current, '''Enrico''' is an extremely hostile entity that attacks any individual within its sight. Its attack formation involves the use of its firearm, which it can arm and aim at rapid, human-surpassing speeds. Dodging '''Enrico's''' bullets has been known to be an incredibly difficult feat to assume. Furthermore, '''Enrico''' has a high awareness, which also hardens the task of staging a surprise attack. However, loud noises are known to distract '''Enrico''', as its mental state appears to be extremely fragile. Lastly, it is theorized that in only four years, '''Enrico''' will transition into the final stage of [[Phenomenon 35|Roth Syndrome]] and fully become a Wretch. == '''Addendum''' == These are all journal entries of '''Enrico''' that have been discovered: {{Dark Document|content=Entry 44 Today I found myself in a new place. Before... all this, I was previously in [[Level 242]]. Then I found a metal surface, something I'd never have expected to find there. And instead of showing it to the other meggies like a normal person, I decided to test my luck and [[Noclipping|noclip]] through its surface. And now, here I am. If this level's dangerous, I guess I deserve it. Anyways, this place looks very weird. Consists of various types of steel structures... with metals and minerals that are built to look... valuable. But as with everything in the Backrooms, trusting beautiful and pretty objects is a death trap. I don't think this is a level previously documented in the database. I might be its discoverer. I guess I never would've expected that designation. I'll add another entry once I explore this area a bit more.}}{{Dark Document|content=Entry 45 Well, I checked the place out a bit more. Found nothing that noteworthy, nothing different, no exits... I found a [[Entity 3|Smiler]] in one of the darker... ''chambers'', though luckily I am armed. Though... I do feel very ''warm''. Not "cozy" warm, but the type of heat that's quite discomforting. It's present the most on the ground; if I had to guess, there's some weird thermal shit going on down there. Anyways, I found a chamber that seems to be less hot than the others. Probably has a natural cooling system somewhere. I think I'll crash here for the night.}}{{Dark Document|content=Entry 46 For reference, it's been approximately 30 days since my last recorded entry. I have been sleeping in this same chamber for a whole month. And now, guess what. It's all gone. My face is gone. I've got no features. I'm practically a [[Entity 9|Faceling]]. Turns out the cooling system was actually a heating system. Probably some natural Backrooms crap that made my face melt off. This situation's getting worse. My [[Object 1|Almond Water]] supplies are running low, I am mentally exhausted, and I still haven't found an exit. I fear this level is going to be my grave. ---- Entry 75 Today, four strangers attempted to steal my shinies. I (garbled) to (garbled) them. No one has the right to steal my shinies, my precious shinies. Sad thing is one of the strangers escaped, and with them went some of my shinies. Sad. I also found a purple shiny chamber. Purple shiny is bad. Scares me.}} == Settlements == As of now, '''Level 828''' does not possess any colonies or outposts, though it did previously. Following '''Enrico''''s situation, the [[M.E.G.]] publicly stated that it wanted nothing to do with the level, which the [[Republic of Aurielle]] took advantage of. However, [[Aurielle]]'s base would later become a massive failure, following the corruption of numerous occupants due to their refusal to follow instructions in avoiding ''Greed''. === Auriellene Base 2-1V3 (Defunct) === * Was established by [[Republic of Aurielle|Aurielle]] in 2019 for resource extraction and geopolitical advantage. * Was located one mile north (using the [[The Frontrooms|Frontroom's]] cardinal points) of the area where all wanderers commonly find themselves. * Had approximately 50 members, 20 of whom were official staff. * Survived for 9 months. * Was later dismantled due to the ''Greed'' phenomenon. * Its ruins can be found while passing through '''Level 828'''. Following Base 2-1V3's failure, establishing any colonies or outposts within '''Level 828''' has been deemed infeasible and a waste of time and resources. == Entrances and Exits == === Entrances === * Referencing the discovery method used by '''Enrico''', no-clipping through a steel wall or surface in [[Level 242]] (or on occasion [[Level 906]] and [[Level 960]]) will lead to '''Level 828'''. === '''Exits''' === '''Level 828''' currently possesses a single exit. * No-clipping through the ground of an Empty Chamber may send one to [[Level 322]]. {{Redirect|Prev=827|Next=829}}{{AuthorInfo|author1=[[User:Granazdi|Granazdi]]|author2=[[User:LuminousFiction|LuminousFiction]] (SPaG Correction)|author3=[[User:First Explorer|Nickyster]] (Revisions)|message=I liked how this level turned out,i hope it receives a rewrite in the future,thank you Luminous Fiction for helping me with SPaG errors}} [[Category:Cluster I]] [[Category:Dangerous]] [[Category:Levels]] [[Category:Mental Hazards]] [[Category:Objects]] [[Category:Environmental Hazards]]
